requirements.label: Use Next.js latest stable runtime
requirements.evidence: `Spec Drivers`; `Proposed Architecture` (`Next.js` App Router).

requirements.label: Use Supabase official client libraries
requirements.evidence: `Spec Drivers`; `Delivery Phases > 1. Foundation and repo scaffolding` (`Supabase client setup`).
requirements.gap: The plan commits to Supabase but does not explicitly require the official Supabase client libraries.

requirements.label: Ship `.env.example` with required variables
requirements.evidence: `Target File Layout` (`.env.example`); `Testing Strategy` (`confirm .env.example completeness`).

requirements.label: Ignore `.env*` secrets except example
requirements.evidence: `Settings And Backup` (`Keep API keys out of version control`); `Testing Strategy` (`no secret leakage`).
requirements.gap: It never explicitly calls for `.gitignore` rules that exclude `.env*` while preserving `.env.example`.

requirements.label: Configure build through env without code edits
requirements.evidence: `Spec Drivers` (`env-only setup`); `Delivery Phases > 1. Foundation and repo scaffolding` (`environment contract`); `Definition Of Done` (`runs via env configuration only`).

requirements.label: Keep secrets out of repo and server-only
requirements.evidence: `Settings And Backup`; `Cross-Cutting Decisions` (`server boundaries to protect secrets`); `Testing Strategy` (`browser-only anon key usage`).

requirements.label: Provide app, test, reset command scripts
requirements.evidence: `Target File Layout` (`package.json`, `reset-namespace.ts`); `Testing Strategy`.
requirements.gap: The reset path is planned, but start-app and test-script deliverables are not explicitly called out as required commands.

requirements.label: Include repeatable schema evolution artifacts
requirements.evidence: `Target File Layout` (`supabase/migrations/`); `Delivery Phases > 1. Foundation and repo scaffolding`; `Testing Strategy` (`repeatable migrations`).

requirements.label: Use one stable namespace per build
requirements.evidence: `Domain Model And Data Responsibilities` (`(namespace_id, user_id)`); `Definition Of Done` (`namespace-safe persistence`).
requirements.gap: The plan enforces namespacing but does not explicitly state that one stable namespace is fixed for the lifetime of each build/run.

requirements.label: Isolate namespaces and scope destructive resets
requirements.evidence: `Target File Layout` (`reset-namespace.ts`); `Delivery Phases > 2. Data model, persistence, and merge engine`; `Testing Strategy` (`namespace-scoped reset`).

requirements.label: Attach every user record to `user_id`
requirements.evidence: `Spec Drivers` (`strict (namespace_id, user_id) isolation`); `Domain Model And Data Responsibilities`.

requirements.label: Partition persisted data by namespace and user
requirements.source: infra_rider_prd.md > 4.3 Relationship between namespace and user
requirements.evidence: `Domain Model And Data Responsibilities` (`The effective storage partition is (namespace_id, user_id)`).

requirements.label: Support documented dev auth injection, prod-gated
requirements.source: infra_rider_prd.md > 5.1 Auth is not required to be "real" in benchmark mode
requirements.evidence: `Delivery Phases > 1. Foundation and repo scaffolding` (`benchmark-friendly identity injection`); `Target File Layout` (`src/server/auth/`).
requirements.gap: The plan does not explicitly require documentation of the dev auth path or spell out production gating.

requirements.label: Real OAuth later needs no schema redesign
requirements.source: infra_rider_prd.md > 5.2 Migration to real OAuth must be straightforward
requirements.evidence: `Target File Layout` (`src/server/auth/`); `Domain Model And Data Responsibilities` (`explicit identity context`).
requirements.gap: It implies an auth abstraction but does not explicitly say real OAuth can be added later without schema redesign.

requirements.label: Keep backend as persisted source of truth
requirements.evidence: `Spec Drivers` (`server-side source of truth`); `Cross-Cutting Decisions` (`writes go through server boundaries`); `Definition Of Done`.

requirements.label: Make client cache safe to discard
requirements.evidence: `Proposed Architecture` (`Only durable data should be persisted`); `Cross-Cutting Decisions`.
requirements.gap: The plan implies disposable client state, but it never explicitly states that clearing local storage or reinstalling must be safe.

requirements.label: Avoid Docker requirement for cloud-agent compatibility
requirements.evidence: none
requirements.gap: Docker avoidance and hosted-Supabase cloud-agent compatibility are not addressed anywhere in the plan.

requirements.label: Overlay saved user data on every show appearance
requirements.evidence: `Product Breakdown` (`user's version of a show always wins`); `Domain Model And Data Responsibilities` (`Show` overlays public and user-owned fields).

requirements.label: Support visible statuses plus hidden `Next`
requirements.evidence: `Domain Model And Data Responsibilities` (`myStatus`, `myInterest`); `Feature-By-Feature Plan > Collection Home`.
requirements.gap: The status model is covered broadly, but the hidden `Next` state is never called out.

requirements.label: Map Interested/Excited chips to Later interest
requirements.evidence: `Feature-By-Feature Plan > Collection Home` (`Excited`, `Interested` groups); `Delivery Phases > 3. Collection and search backbone` (`interest save`).
requirements.gap: The plan does not explicitly state that the Interested/Excited chips map to `Later` plus an interest level.

requirements.label: Support free-form multi-tag personal tag library
requirements.evidence: `Domain Model And Data Responsibilities` (`myTags`); `Delivery Phases > 3. Collection and search backbone` (`tag-derived navigation`); `Target File Layout` (`TagManager`).

requirements.label: Define collection membership by assigned status
requirements.evidence: `Domain Model And Data Responsibilities` (`Show` carries `myStatus`); `Delivery Phases > 3. Collection and search backbone`.
requirements.gap: It plans status-driven saves, but it does not explicitly define collection membership as presence of a status.

requirements.label: Save shows from status, interest, rating, tagging

requirements.label: Default save to Later/Interested except rating-save Done
requirements.evidence: `Delivery Phases > 3. Collection and search backbone` (`rating-to-save as Done`; `tag-to-save as Later + Interested`).

requirements.label: Removing status deletes show and all My Data
requirements.evidence: `Feature-By-Feature Plan > Show Detail` (`destructive removal behavior behind confirmation`); `Cross-Cutting Decisions` (`remove-from-collection semantics`).

requirements.gap: The merge engine is planned, but the specific re-add flow that restores prior My Data while refreshing public metadata is not spelled out.

requirements.label: Track per-field My Data modification timestamps
requirements.evidence: `Domain Model And Data Responsibilities` (`user-owned fields resolve by per-field timestamps`); `Cross-Cutting Decisions` (`merge timestamps`).

requirements.gap: Timestamp use is planned for merge behavior and freshness, but sorting and sync use cases are not called out explicitly.

top_gaps.label: Use one stable namespace per build. The plan enforces `(namespace_id, user_id)` partitioning, but if namespace lifetime is not fixed per build, benchmark runs can bleed into one another and destructive resets stop being trustworthy

Removing status deletes show and all My Data. The plan covers confirmation and removal semantics, but without an explicit full-data wipe contract, users can end up with ghost ratings, tags, or Scoop data after removal

Re-add preserves My Data and refreshes public data. The merge engine is planned, but the re-add behavior is not named, which risks losing the user's prior relationship history when a show is saved again

Emit `commentary` plus exact `showList` contract. The list string format is specified, but the enclosing response object is not, so the Ask UI contract could fail even if recommendation text is otherwise good

Enforce shared AI guardrails across all surfaces. Persona consistency is planned, but without explicit shared guardrails, surfaces can diverge on spoiler safety, domain limits, honesty, and recommendation integrity

narrative.overall_posture: The plan is structurally sound and covers most of the product's major systems, but it stops short of full benchmark readiness because several critical rules are handled by implication rather than explicit planning language. The weighted coverage score is 77.3%, which is solid for architecture and sequencing but still leaves meaningful execution risk in benchmark-isolation details and AI behavioral contracts.
narrative.strength_clusters: The strongest coverage is in Collection Home & Search, Show Detail & Relationship UX, Person Detail, and Settings & Export. Those sections are concrete about feature sequencing, save triggers, detail-page ordering, export packaging, and the main user journeys, and they are reinforced by explicit testing coverage for merge rules, fallback behavior, and namespace scoping.
narrative.weakness_clusters: The gaps cluster in two places. First, the benchmark runtime/isolation area is only partially specified on operational details such as official Supabase libraries, dev-auth documentation, stable namespace lifetime, disposable client cache expectations, and Docker-free cloud-agent compatibility. Second, the AI surface contracts are weaker on exactness than on intent: the plan captures shared infrastructure and quality goals, but several Ask, Scoop, concept-format, and voice requirements remain underspecified.
narrative.risk_assessment: If this plan were executed as-is, the app would likely land the major pages and data model but still miss benchmark-critical edge behavior. A QA reviewer would probably notice the gaps first in namespace/test isolation rules and in AI interaction polish: chat starter prompts missing, Ask contracts not fully machine-safe, voice guardrails drifting across surfaces, and some removal/re-add data semantics behaving inconsistently under real use.
narrative.remediation_guidance: The remaining planning work is mostly specification tightening, not a wholesale rewrite. The next revision should add explicit benchmark-operational acceptance criteria for env/gitignore/scripts/auth/isolation/cache behavior; make the save/remove/re-add lifecycle fully explicit; and turn the AI sections from high-level intent into exact contracts for response schemas, guardrails, voice pillars, concept formatting, and surface-specific context inputs.
